title: arum orientale var. longispathum
intro: Arum orientale var. longispathum, also known as the long-spathed arum lily or Dracunculus longispathum, is a plant species within the Arum genus, characterized by its distinctive and sometimes fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, where its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits associated with Arum orientale var. longispathum include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating digestive problems and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
